conditions: At least one named boundary materially differs between the workloads.
decision_context: Prefer the smallest account separation that satisfies the explicit boundary requirements.
exclusions: Do not prescribe a universal account count or split accounts without a stated benefit.

decision_criterion: Choose account separation when isolation, ownership clarity, quota independence, billing clarity, or blast-radius containment outweighs added account-governance overhead.

source_references.locator: Benefits of using multiple AWS accounts
source_references.supported_proposition: Account separation is recommended when business and governance boundaries differ.

statement: Separate workloads into different AWS accounts when isolation, ownership, quota, billing, or adverse-event containment requirements are materially different.
